key_points: Boiler explosion in one dorm made students reconsider on-campus living
Students pressured into off-campus leases with aggressive sales tactics
COVID forced dorm evacuation with less than one week notice
key_quotes: After the boiler explosion in one of the halls and seeing cheaper options for living in the area, I'm re-thinking my decision
They pressured me to sign the lease by the end of today because prices could go up as soon as tomorrow
I checked out the reviews and a lot of them were bad

key_points: Greek life exists and is active enough to have dedicated sorority chefs
Reno's outdoor access—skiing, Tahoe—is a genuine social draw
Campus social scene is not a major talking point; overshadowed by location
key_quotes: Some of the best memories of my life were made in Reno during ski trips. You will have an absolute blast
Now that you don't have as many student loans you can gamble at the casinos in Reno during free time

key_points: Sorority dining praised—chef Kevin knows every member's name and preferences
No specific complaints about general campus dining in the data
Institutional dining quality appears variable; sorority model is exceptional outlier
key_quotes: Kevin knowing all the women in the sorority's names and making each plate to their tastes and requests is amazing
He's their 'dad away from home,' making sure their bellies are fed so their brains can learn

key_points: No dedicated mental health support resources mentioned by students
Transfer stress and housing anxiety are recurring themes without institutional solutions
Students navigating major life changes at UNR largely without noted campus support
key_quotes: Crossing the state line is the first thing worrying me
I'm struggling... I can't take it

key_points: Merit scholarships are transparent and calculator-based—you know exactly what to expect
Presidential scholarship cutoffs are strict; no exceptions made for borderline cases
Nevada Bound program offers near-free attendance for qualifying in-state students
key_quotes: I have barely made the cutoff for the presidential scholarship... if I were to email them and ask if they could make an exception, would they?
UNR is a fairly solid school... its obviously no T20, but its not like you're going to a trash school. And you can go into both UNLV and UNR with FAFSA and scholarships with extra money given to me
Have you done the Nevada Bound program UNR offers?

key_points: Non-STEM courses reported as highly politicized by at least one student
Engineering programs are ABET accredited and considered solid, not exceptional
Class enrollment system has pitfalls—students accidentally dropped from required courses
key_quotes: Professors make sure class is highly politicized, especially if it isn't a STEM course, so be prepared
I feel scammed. Be sure to drop before the early deadline or they keep 100%
CSE Senior at UNR here, being within the top 5 on a waitlist isn't that bad at all

key_points: COVID housing evacuation gave students less than one week to move out
Drop deadline policy penalizes students 100% if missed—no grace period noted
Transfer credit requirements added unexpectedly by advisors, causing stress
key_quotes: We have been asked to leave dorms, to move out and turn keys in. We have less than a week to do this
I feel scammed. Be sure to drop before the early deadline or they keep 100%
I met with a counselor at UNR and was told I have to take 2 more Spanish classes

key_points: Campus is modern and visually impressive—widely described as a hidden gem
Reno itself described as 'dangerous/dirty' by one prospective student post
Proximity to Lake Tahoe and ski country is a genuine lifestyle advantage
key_quotes: It has a super modern campus in the high-desert mountains (google it, it looks actually amazing) and the wolf pack is a sick mascot
Only problems are that its in Reno (dangerous/dirty)
I was admitted here in 2016 but... I wish I stayed at UNR way friendlier, nice campus and ethical

key_points: Engineering grads described as doing 'fine' but school lacks strong recruiting reputation
Tesla Gigafactory proximity in Sparks cited as real internship opportunity for engineers
CS degree value seen as adequate but school is not a target for top tech employers
key_quotes: Nevada isn't a big engineering research school, but it's ABET accredited and their grads do fine. Just make sure to network and seek out internships
I did research and found out that they have a gigafactory in Sparks, NV which is very close to the college and would provide a great opportunity for an internship
UNR isn't that highly ranked [for CS]

key_points: Free or near-free attendance for strong Nevada residents is the school's biggest selling point
Multiple students chose UNR over $200k+ debt at brand-name schools and felt great about it
Out-of-state students can access WUE rates (150% of in-state), making it affordable regionally
key_quotes: It costs so much money and I couldn't see myself being able to handle 200k+ debt for my undergrad alone... I'm planning on double majoring as an engineer and music tech
No school—not Harvard, none of them—is worth $300k
It's a popular choice in my area (in NorCal, about two hours from UNR). They do offer the WUE tuition rates

key_points: Strong 'smart financial choice' energy—students arrive pragmatic, not passionate
Friendly campus culture with outdoor lifestyle appeal for the right student
Perception gap: students know it's not prestigious but most seem genuinely satisfied
key_quotes: I did not see myself going up to UNR... it was just so expensive [elsewhere]. Smart financial moves will save you so much agony
UNR is a very pleasant place. Don't discount because it might be close to home
It's an up and coming school and Reno has a lot of outdoor opportunities

key_points: **Drop Policy:** Missing early drop deadline means losing 100% of tuition paid
**Administration:** Advisors added surprise course requirements for transfer students mid-process
**Politics in Class:** Non-STEM courses explicitly flagged as highly politicized by a current student
key_quotes: I feel scammed. Be sure to drop before the early deadline or they keep 100%
Professors make sure class is highly politicized, especially if it isn't a STEM course
I met with a counselor at UNR and was told I have to take 2 more Spanish classes [despite AP credit]

key_points: BS-MD program exists exclusively for Nevada residents—low-key path to med school
Undergrad research opportunities are cited as genuinely accessible vs. larger schools
National Merit Finalists can leverage UNR for significant merit aid stacking
key_quotes: My state has one [BS-MD program] at UNR that only Nevada residents can apply to. It doesn't seem too difficult for me to get in
I know I would be able to do research and stand out at UNR which I have heard is quite important for medical admissions
UNR is a fairly solid school... its obviously no T20, but UNR has a solid engineering program and has lots of opportunities for undergrad research
